concepts.name: Test Without Assertion
concepts.problem_description: Test executes code but never verifies the outcome - test function completes without any assertion call
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: tree-sitter to detect test function nodes; traverse body to verify at least one assertion call exists (expect(), assert*, should, assertThat, pytest assert statements)
false_positive_conditions: Tests that return a value to be verified externally
Tests that use mock verification instead of assertions
Tests that expect exceptions (should use expect(() => ...).toThrow())

concepts.name: Test Using Sleep
concepts.problem_description: Test using time.sleep() or equivalent - causes slow tests and flaky behavior due to timing dependencies
concepts.detection_strategy: Pattern match via tree-sitter: detect sleep(duration), delay(), wait_for_seconds(), Thread.sleep() calls in test functions
false_positive_conditions: Sleep in setUp/tearDown for resource cleanup timing
Sleep in performance tests measuring timing behavior
Sleeps in integration tests waiting for external services

concepts.name: Test Skipped Without Reason
concepts.problem_description: Test skipped without explanation - @unittest.skip, describe.skip, or it.skip without reason string
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: detect skip decorators with empty reason string; pattern @skip, @skipIf, @skipUnless without message; JavaScript: describe.skip(), it.skip(), test.skip() with no explanation
false_positive_conditions: Skip with detailed ticket number or explanation
Skip conditionally based on environment variables
Skip for known platform-specific issues

concepts.name: Test Method Naming Convention Violation
concepts.problem_description: Test method not following naming convention - should start with test_ prefix but doesn't
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: detect method definitions in test classes; verify name starts with 'test'; pattern def test_*, async def test_*; flag methods in Test* classes that don't start with test
false_positive_conditions: Helper methods that support tests (not test methods themselves)
setUp/tearDown methods
Class methods like __init__

concepts.name: Complex Test Fixture Setup
concepts.problem_description: Test fixture setup too complex - setUp with excessive lines indicates high coupling and maintenance burden
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: count lines in setUp/setUpClass fixture methods; flag setUp methods exceeding 20 lines of code; check for excessive mocking or complex object construction
false_positive_conditions: Fixtures that initialize complex domain objects
Database migration fixtures
Integration test fixtures requiring multiple services

concepts.name: Multiple Assertions In Single Test
concepts.problem_description: Multiple assertions in one test - test should have single logical assertion for better isolation and diagnostics
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: count assertion statements in test function body; flag tests with >3 assertion calls; detect assertTrue/assertEqual chains
false_positive_conditions: Assertions on related properties of same object
Assertions in loops checking multiple elements
Setup assertions that guard test prerequisites

concepts.name: Duplicated Test Method
concepts.problem_description: Duplicated test method - identical or near-identical test code that should be refactored or parametrized
concepts.detection_strategy: AST comparison: detect test functions with identical or highly similar bodies; similarity hashing of test function bodies; flag test methods with same assertions and same setup
false_positive_conditions: Tests that verify same behavior with different inputs (parametrized tests)
Tests with same intent but different assertions
Tests for error handling vs success paths

concepts.name: Test Using Random Values
concepts.problem_description: Test using random values - creates non-deterministic and flaky tests that fail intermittently
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: detect random.*, Math.random(), random.randint() calls in test body; flag use of random module, numpy.random, java.util.Random in tests; check for @RepeatedTest with random seed
false_positive_conditions: Random used to generate test data that's then explicitly asserted
Tests that use fixed seeds (random.seed(42))
Property-based tests intentionally using random

concepts.name: Test Without Describe Block
concepts.problem_description: Test without describe block - tests should be grouped in describe containers for organization
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: detect it/test calls not nested inside describe blocks; flag root-level it(), test(), describe() without parent describe; check for test入れ子構造
false_positive_conditions: Top-level integration tests
Tests in __tests__ directories with descriptive file names
Single test files that don't require grouping

concepts.name: Assertions Count Mismatch
concepts.problem_description: expect.assertions() count mismatch - declared count doesn't match actual assertion count in async tests
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: detect expect.assertions(N) calls; count actual expect() calls in async test; dataflow track assertion count; flag mismatch between declared and actual assertion count
false_positive_conditions: expect.assertions in test files that use only expect() without count verification
Tests that use expect.hasAssertions() instead
Conditional assertions based on platform/features

concepts.name: Nested Test Hooks
concepts.problem_description: beforeAll/afterAll in nested describe - hooks should only be at top level to avoid scoping issues
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: detect beforeAll/afterAll/beforeEach/afterEach in nested describe blocks; flag hooks inside describe blocks that are themselves nested; verify hook scope is only top-level
false_positive_conditions: Hooks in nested describe for specific test groups
Hooks for conditionally grouped tests
Hooks in test classes with inheritance

concepts.name: Mock Implementation Confusion
concepts.problem_description: mockImplementation used with mockReturnValue on same mock - should pick one approach
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: detect mock objects with both mockImplementation and mockReturnValue calls; flag same mock with conflicting return value specifications; check for .mockImplementation().mockReturnValue() chains
false_positive_conditions: Different methods on same mock (one uses implementation, another uses return value)
mockImplementation for some calls, mockReturnValue for others
Conditional mock behavior

concepts.name: SpyOn Not Restored
concepts.problem_description: spyOn with original implementation not restored - causes test pollution across tests
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: detect spyOn calls without corresponding jest.restoreAllMocks(); track spyOn calls and verify cleanup in afterEach/tearDown; flag missing mock restoration pattern
false_positive_conditions: spyOn on jest.fn() mocks that auto-cleanup
Mocks created with jest.spyOn + mockImplementation in same test
Tests using jest.clearAllMocks() instead

concepts.name: Act Wrapper Missing
concepts.problem_description: act() wrapper missing for async state updates in React tests - causes 'not wrapped in act' warnings
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: detect async test functions that call setState/useState without act() wrapper; flag fireEvent/userEvent calls in async tests without act(); check for waitFor/waitForElementToBeRemoved without act()
false_positive_conditions: Sync state updates that don't require act()
Tests using @testing-library/react waitFor which handles act internally
Enzyme shallow render tests

concepts.name: Weak Assertion Style
concepts.problem_description: Weak assertion style - toBeTruthy vs toBe(true) causes ambiguity and less precise failure messages
concepts.detection_strategy: AST-based: detect expect(x).toBeTruthy() and suggest toBe(true) for boolean checks; flag toBeFalsy() that should be toBe(false); detect assertTrue(x) that should assertEqual(x, expected)
false_positive_conditions: toBeTruthy for checking object existence
toBeFalsy for checking null/undefined
Assertions on functions that return boolean-like values
